threadsafe_note: sqlite3_threadsafe() is compile-time pinned to 1 by libsqlite3-sys-0.30.1 build.rs:136 (-DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=1). Cannot change at runtime; per SQLite header sqlite3.h:249-252 it returns the compile-time value regardless of sqlite3_config. Not asserted as a B.1 gate per UPDATE 2026-05-03.
sqlite3_config_return_code_note: 0 = SQLITE_OK. This is the §5 differentiator: the §5 prior revert returned SQLITE_MISUSE = 21 silently because the call was placed inside register_sqlite_vec_extension's Once block after rusqlite had already triggered sqlite3_initialize. B.1 places init_sqlite_runtime at open_locked head BEFORE register_sqlite_vec_extension and BEFORE any Connection::open, so sqlite3_initialize has not yet run and sqlite3_config succeeds. Verified by tests/multithread_wiring.rs assertion sqlite_runtime_config_rc() == Some(0).
decision_rule: KEEP iff concurrent_median_ms <= 80 AND speedup >= 5.0x AND AC-018 green; INCONCLUSIVE in 80-100ms band (recapture + stack B.3); REVERT iff concurrent_median_ms > 115 OR AC-018 red. Per A.4 OVERRIDE update 2026-05-03.
decision_rule_evaluation: concurrent_median = 120.6 ms > 115 ms threshold (REVERT trigger). Speedup 1.526x is essentially unchanged from A.1 baseline 1.58x (within 1 stddev: stddev_speedup=0.025). Sequential effectively unchanged (+1.1%). Concurrent shifted +4.9% which is ~1.7 stddev — borderline but on the wrong side of the gate. Strict rule: REVERT.
kill_criteria_note: Kill rule (B.1 + B.3 stacked < 10% drop ⇒ promote D.1) requires B.3 also to land. B.1 alone shows 0% drop (mild regression within noise). Ready to evaluate after B.3 or in light of C.1 hypothesis below.
data_for_pivot: APPLIED-BUT-DIDN'T-HELP. sqlite3_config(SQLITE_CONFIG_MULTITHREAD) was correctly invoked at open_locked head BEFORE any Connection::open; rc=SQLITE_OK=0 (vs §5's silent 21). init_sqlite_runtime is OnceLock-cached (idempotent across reopens). Yet the AC-020 ratio is unchanged (1.526x vs A.1 1.58x; concurrent 120.6 vs 115 ms — within noise). Interpretation: runtime threading-mode downgrade from SERIALIZED (compile-pinned 1) to MULTITHREAD does NOT relieve the bottleneck. Two explanations consistent with the A.1 hot-symbol set (__aarch64_swp4_rel/_cas4_acq dominant in concurrent): (a) per-connection mutex elimination requires compile-time SQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 + SQLITE_NO_MUTEX, not runtime config — promotes C.1 to next; (b) the contended primitive is the WAL-pager spinlock which is unaffected by threading-mode flag — promotes D.1 (per-conn lookaside or different reader architecture). Recommend running C.1 first (cheaper to test the hypothesis: rebuild libsqlite3-sys with -DSQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 and re-measure). If C.1 still shows no improvement, kill the mutex track and pivot to D.1.
unexpected_observations: The 5 runs were tightly clustered (concurrent stddev 2.98 ms) with no outliers; AC-018 stayed green; AC-017 stayed green. The B.1 intervention is provably wired (config_rc=0 cached in OnceLock, idempotent across reopens), eliminating the §5-class silent-no-op failure mode. The fact that wiring works but perf is flat is itself a high-confidence signal that the runtime threading-mode flag is NOT the right knob — the dominant SQLite primitives we observed in A.1 (__aarch64_swp4_rel, __aarch64_cas4_acq, ___pthread_mutex_lock) are not the SQLite global-mutex which CONFIG_MULTITHREAD relaxes; they are likely WAL-pager spinlocks and per-connection mutexes that only SQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 (compile-time, C.1 territory) can disable. Pre-existing compile errors in tests/{compatibility,cursors,lifecycle_observability}.rs are A.0 baseline (noted in A.1 pre_flight_notes); not B.1's regression.
next_phase_recommendation_rationale: B.1 ruled out the runtime threading-mode hypothesis with a clean, observable gate (config_rc=0 yet ratio flat). Two surviving hypotheses: C.1 (compile-time SQLITE_THREADSAFE=2 / SQLITE_NO_MUTEX rebuild — directly addresses the per-connection mutex symbols dominating the concurrent flame) and D.1 (architectural — per-conn lookaside or alt reader topology). C.1 is a smaller-radius intervention and tests a more specific hypothesis with the same evidence stack; recommend C.1 first. If C.1 also lands flat, kill the mutex track per the A.4 kill criterion and promote D.1.

tests_run_summary.perf_gates: AC-017 green, AC-018 green, AC-020 long-run failed (expected — perf gate not met)
compile_errors_pre_existing: tests/lifecycle_observability.rs (test file with compile errors at lines 414/446/453/489)
tests_run_summary.compile_errors_pre_existing_note: A.0/A.1 baseline; documented in dev/plan/runs/A1-perf-capture-output.json pre_flight_notes.build_note. Not introduced by B.1.
